note: Milestone 1 gate O (foundry/docs/jini-port/extraction-plan.md §4.3 / §8 task 1). Maps upstream OD daemon paths ('apps/daemon/...', mirrored here under 'foundry/integrations/open-design/daemon/...' by scripts/patch-canary.ts's transformUpstreamPath) to 'product-owned' | 'delegated-to-jini'. A patch touching a 'delegated-to-jini' path must not land until the equivalent fix also lands in the cited @jini/* package. THIS IS A FIRST-PASS, NOT EXHAUSTIVE EXTRACTION — entries were pulled from packages/*/source-map.md 'File map'/classification tables that cite concrete apps/daemon/... origin paths, scoped to daemon-relevant packages only (frontend/web-scoped packages — ui, chat-react, renderers-react — were skipped per task scope). Classification rule used: if a source-map.md row names at least one concretely-ported symbol/function/route from that exact origin path (even a partial/small subset of a larger file), the path is 'delegated-to-jini'; if a path was only read, referenced, or classified as 'plausibly generic' but nothing was actually ported yet, it is 'product-owned' (current state — nothing in @jini/* depends on it yet). Multi-thousand-line files where the source-map.md explicitly says the port is a fresh composition 'not a lift' (e.g. apps/daemon/src/server.ts, apps/daemon/src/runtimes/{runs,start-chat-run,chat-run-lifecycle}.ts) were deliberately excluded as too ambiguous for file-level classification. The fixture path 'apps/daemon/src/routes/health.ts' is synthetic (see reference/fixtures/README.md — no network access to real upstream history) but is classified 'delegated-to-jini' on its merits: a health/status-check route is exactly the category of generic daemon infra @jini/http's daemon-status.ts already ports from apps/daemon/src/routes/daemon.ts's GET /api/daemon/status. Future sessions should extend this manifest as more of apps/daemon/ gets read/ported — see 'packages' below for which source-map.md to re-check per group, and extraction-plan.md §4 for the eventual goal of covering every upstream daemon path.

core.detail: Flat daemon primitives (redact/api-token-auth/origin-validation) genericized verbatim from apps/daemon/src/*.ts.

http.detail: Six shared http/ modules + local-daemon-request.ts + api-security-middleware.ts + route-registration-guard.ts ported. tool-request-auth.ts and oauth-result-page.ts explicitly NOT ported (OD-coupled). The 'routes/ classification (32 files)' table is the authoritative source for every apps/daemon/src/routes/*.ts verdict here: only daemon.ts's GET /api/daemon/status + POST /api/daemon/shutdown pair was actually ported (into src/daemon-status.ts) this round; the other 31 files (2 GENERIC-but-unported, 14 MIXED-but-unported, 15 OD-PRODUCT) all remain product-owned until a future port task lands them for real — see that table for per-file reasoning (hardcoded domains, @open-design/contracts coupling, etc).

node-host.detail: daemon-startup.ts's normalizeDaemonBindHost/closeHttpServer ported verbatim; CLI-flag parsing half not ported. apps/daemon/src/server.ts deliberately excluded from this manifest — node-host's own source-map.md calls create-local-node-daemon.ts 'new composition, not a lift' of server.ts, and the origin file is thousands of lines of mostly product-specific wiring; too coarse to classify at file granularity with confidence.

platform.detail: home-expansion.ts, sandbox-mode.ts (-> sandbox-env.ts), daemon-paths.ts (-> resource-paths.ts), terminals.ts (-> terminal.ts), plugins/plugin-asset-cache.ts (-> asset-cache.ts) + its test file all ported. storage/aws-sigv4.ts and storage/project-storage.ts (-> aws-sigv4.ts/blob-storage.ts, 2026-07-18 addition) also landed here: project-storage.ts's ProjectStorage/LocalProjectStorage/S3ProjectStorage classes ported (only the OD-env-var resolveProjectStorage() factory was dropped) — note this supersedes an earlier, now-stale 'not ported' note in packages/sqlite/source-map.md's own history.

mcp.detail: core/{config,oauth,tokens,install-info}.ts and agent-install/install.ts ported faithfully. client/client.ts: only small product-neutral primitives (createMcpIdleExitController, extractRelativeRefs, isTextualMime) were salvaged out of an otherwise-dropped OD-product 'od mcp' stdio server — classified delegated-to-jini because those salvaged functions do live on in @jini/mcp, but most of the file's own logic is not represented there.

artifacts.detail: manifest.ts, create.ts (-> store.ts), publication-guard.ts, stub-guard.ts, text-suppression.ts (also independently re-cited in packages/agent-runtime/source-map.md as acp/text-suppression.ts) all ported with real logic carried over (de-branded/generalized). runtime-compat.ts is product-owned: the source-map.md is explicit that the origin's actual logic (a Motion-CDN bug fix) was NOT ported, only a generic injection-point type was invented — no origin symbol survives in @jini/artifacts.

deploy.detail: apps/daemon/src/deploy.ts (2005 lines): the large majority (types, naming, reachability polling, the full Vercel + Cloudflare Pages deploy/DNS flow) was ported/genericized into @jini/deploy; only the OD-project-file-tree-walking family (buildDeployFilePlan et al) and the persisted-config-file convention were dropped. Note this is the underlying deploy *engine* file, distinct from the OD-side route file apps/daemon/src/routes/deploy.ts (product-owned, see http group), which only calls into it.

agent-runtime.detail: role-marker-guard.ts and copilot-stream.ts ported verbatim. pi-rpc.ts, connectionTest.ts, and mcp-oauth.ts are each large multi-hundred/thousand-line OD-coupled files from which only small, explicitly-named pure functions were vendored (parsePiModels; validateBaseUrlResolved+redactSecrets; PKCE/authorize-URL/token-exchange+PendingAuthCache) — classified delegated-to-jini on the strength of those confirmed ports, though most of each origin file's own logic remains OD-only.

cli.detail: apps/daemon/src/cli.ts (9,885 lines) is overwhelmingly a thin HTTP client over OD's product API and was NOT ported wholesale, but a real, named set of generic primitives was (parseFlags, positionalArgs, the structured-error/exit-code mechanism, postJsonToDaemon, readPromptFromFlags, the CommandRegistry dispatcher pattern, renderUsage) — see packages/cli/src/*.ts. daemon-url.ts's resolveDaemonUrl 4-step resolution order was also ported (implementation swapped to injectable discovery, not OD's sidecar IPC). Every other satellite file cli.ts imports (artifacts-cli.ts, handoff-cli.ts, codex-cli.ts, tools-*-cli.ts, export-cli-*.ts, research/cli-args.ts, design-systems-cli-help.ts, brands-cli-help.ts) is classified OD in the source-map.md's own table with nothing ported — product-owned here.

media.detail: media/models.ts, media/config.ts, media/tasks.ts, media/policy.ts, media/amr-image-staging.ts, and all of media-adapters/{types,capabilities,seed,video}.ts ported (some verbatim, some generalized into port+in-memory-reference-implementation pairs). media/index.ts (the 155KB multi-provider REST dispatch engine) is explicitly NOT ported — product-owned.

sqlite.detail: storage/daemon-db.ts and storage/db-inspect.ts ported verbatim (identity-stripped). The db/ capability-barrel (core/{rows,json}.ts, connection/connection.ts, schema/migrate.ts [only the 4 generic tables], conversations/, messages/, projects/, agent-sessions/, plus their barrels and the root db/index.ts) ported — each concern module keeps its generic CRUD/logic verbatim while dropping OD-only columns/tables. metrics/index.ts and logging/critique.ts were investigated and found to be 100% Critique Theater (OD feature) telemetry with no generic core — explicitly not ported, product-owned.

registry.detail: All 4 files under apps/daemon/src/registry/ (versioning.ts, static-backend.ts, github-backend.ts, database-backend.ts) ported/retyped against @jini/protocol's registry schema, de-branded ('plugins'/'marketplace' vocabulary -> 'registry'/'entries').
